---
title: "Post-DSCI 525 Self-study Labs"
subtitle: "Self-paced micro-labs to build cloud and pipeline fluency"
---

You finished DSCI 525. You built an ML pipeline end-to-end: data acquisition, cloud infrastructure, distributed compute, deployed API. But 4 weeks is a sprint, and sprints leave gaps.

These labs give you **practice opportunities**. Each one is a focused, 30-60 minute exercise that reinforces one cluster of skills from the course. Clone, run, see results, then extend.

## How to use this repo

```bash
# Install uv (if you don't have it yet)
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

git clone https://github.com/ilyamusabirov/525next-steps.git
cd 525next-steps
uv sync
```

If you prefer conda/pip:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ilyamusabirov/525next-steps.git
cd 525next-steps
pip install .
```

This reads `pyproject.toml` and installs the same dependencies into your current environment.

Then pick a lab. Each has:

1. **Starter code that works immediately**: clone and run, see output in under 5 minutes
2. **Understand prompts**: "what just happened?" questions to check your mental model
3. **Scale/compare steps**: change a parameter, predict the effect, measure, explain
4. **Extend pointers**: ideas that connect to other labs or capstone-scale problems

## The labs

| Lab | What you practice | Cost | Time |
|---|---|---|---|
| [1. Build a Local Data Pipeline](labs/lab1-local-pipeline.qmd) | Pipeline structure, SQL transforms, quality checks, materialization | Free | 30-45 min |
| [2. Run Analytics in the Cloud](labs/lab2-cloud-analytics.qmd) | SSH, S3-compatible reads, resource monitoring, cost tracking | ~\$0.50 | 45-60 min |
| [3. Text Search with Embeddings](labs/lab3-llm-embeddings.qmd) | Embedding API, vector storage, similarity search in SQL | Free | 45-60 min |
| [4. SQL at Scale](labs/lab4-sql-at-scale.qmd) | DuckDB vs SparkSQL boundary, hash table sizing, Spark UI | ~\$1.50 | 60-90 min |
| [Connecting project](project/index.qmd) | Chains all 3: pipeline + validation + embeddings + ML + report | Free locally | 2-3 hours |

## Live demo & infrastructure

The `demos/sql-on-cluster/` directory contains the scripts from the Week 4 live demo: DuckDB on EC2, SparkSQL on EMR, and the boundary where single-machine SQL breaks. Lab 4 guides you through the same scripts with predict-then-run exercises.

See the [Infrastructure page](infra.qmd) for architecture diagrams, SSH access patterns, and reproduction instructions.

## Before you start

::: {.callout-warning icon="false" title="Cost awareness"}
Every lab that touches cloud resources has a **Cloud Economy** box at the top showing expected cost and free alternatives. Set up a billing alarm before your first cloud lab. We show you how.
:::

**Prerequisites**: Python 3.11+, [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/), and [Bruin CLI](https://getbruin.com/install/cli). Labs 2+ need an AWS account (your course account works, or use [AWS Free Tier](https://aws.amazon.com/free/)).
